categories.match: Cold recruiter outreach, job pitches, staffing agencies. Signals: 'role', 'opportunity', 'reach out about a position', 'hiring', compensation bands in body.
categories.draft_template_hint: Polite decline unless the email explicitly mentions total comp >= $150k or a specific role that matches Danny's data-analyst trajectory. Keep under 80 words. Thank them, state current focus, leave door open.

categories.match: LinkedIn connection accepted notifications and first-touch messages from a new connection. Sender is usually 'LinkedIn' or the new connection directly.
categories.draft_template_hint: Warm intro. Acknowledge the connect by name, one line on what Danny is building (systems-thinking, Blue Hill Capital + self-taught data analysis), point to github.com/DimmMak/the-overhaul, offer a free 30-min call. Max 100 words.

categories.match: Substack, Beehiiv, Medium digests, company newsletters, product updates, Revue-style blasts. List-unsubscribe header usually present.
categories.label: Newsletters

categories.match: Purchase confirmations, order receipts, payment notifications (Stripe, Shopify, Amazon, Apple). Sender contains 'no-reply' or 'receipts'.
categories.label: Receipts

categories.match: Personal correspondence from family, close friends, or first-name-basis contacts. Conversational tone, no transactional signals.
Inbound asking about Danny's time, expertise, or offering a paid engagement. Signals: 'consulting', 'work with you', 'hire you', 'project', 'rate'.
categories.draft_template_hint: High priority. Lead with genuine interest in their problem. Offer a free 30-min discovery call with calendar link placeholder {{CAL_LINK}}. Mention github.com/DimmMak/the-overhaul if relevant. Max 120 words.

categories.match: Automated alerts from systems Danny owns: GitHub Actions failures/warnings, Vercel deploy notifications, CI/CD pipeline status, security/auth notices (new login warnings, password resets, 2FA), DNS/certificate expiry warnings, uptime monitors. Sender is usually 'notifications@github.com', 'no-reply@vercel.com', 'verify@x.com', 'security-noreply@*', or similar machine-generated.
categories.notes: NEVER draft replies — these are no-reply senders. Always flag for human. Dedupe: if a later alert for the same resource shows success, supersede earlier failure alerts in the triage report.

categories.match: Phishing indicators: urgency language ('act now', 'verify immediately'), wire transfer requests, sender domain mismatched with claimed brand (e.g., 'PayPal' from a .ru domain), generic greetings with specific asks, shortened URLs, display-name spoofing a known contact but different email address.
categories.notes: NEVER draft replies. Flag loudly. If display name matches a Danny contact but email domain differs — flag as SPOOF-SUSPECTED.
